title: Calculating stamp duty
problem: A car is purchased for $\$24{,}000$. Stamp duty is charged at 3% of the purchase price. Calculate the stamp duty payable.

steps.instruction: Write the stamp duty rate as a decimal.
Multiply the purchase price by the rate.

title: Total on-road costs
problem: A car has a purchase price of $\$30{,}000$. Stamp duty is 3% of the purchase price, registration costs $\$380$ and CTP insurance costs $\$650$. Calculate the total on-road cost.

title: Comparing outright purchase with a loan
problem: A car has a cash price of $\$24{,}000$. A loan option requires a $\$4{,}000$ deposit and 48 monthly repayments of $\$490$. Calculate the total interest paid and express it as a percentage of the cash price.

steps.instruction: Calculate the total of all monthly repayments.
Add the deposit to find the total amount paid under the loan.
Find the interest paid by subtracting the cash price from the total loan cost.
Express the interest as a percentage of the cash price.

answer: The total interest paid is $\$3{,}520$, which is approximately $14.67\%$ of the cash price. The loan option costs $\$3{,}520$ more than paying cash.
